seoTitle: Venus Bay vs Vermont South — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Venus Bay vs Vermont South: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Venus Bay and Vermont South.
heading: Venus Bay vs Vermont South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Venus Bay, Victoria and Vermont South, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Venus Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Vermont South (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Venus Bay or Vermont South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Venus Bay has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Vermont South scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Venus Bay scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Vermont South scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Venus Bay or Vermont South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Venus Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
